The coordinates of `interpolator x y t` are `(1 - t) * x i + t * y i`.
The derivative with respect to `t` of `(1 - t) * x i + t * y i` is `y i - x i`.

So by the chain rule, the derivative of `f` at `interpolator x y t` is `∑ i, (y i - x i) * nth_partial i f (interpolator x y t)`.
Which is `∑ i, (y i - x i) * nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y)`.

        apply Differentiable.mul
        · -- Show that (fun t : ℝ => (y i - x i)) is differentiable
          apply differentiable_const
        · -- Show that (fun t : ℝ => nth_partial i f ((1 - t) • x + t • y)) is differentiable
          apply Differentiable.nthPartial
          · -- Show that (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t) • x + t • y) is differentiable
            apply Differentiable.add
            · -- Show that (fun t : ℝ => (1 - t) • x) is differentiable
              apply Differentiable.smul
              · -- Show that (fun t : ℝ => 1 - t) is differentiable
                apply differentiable_sub
                · -- Show that (fun t : ℝ => 1) is differentiable
                  apply differentiable_const
                · -- Show that (fun t : ℝ => t) is differentiable
                  apply differentiable_id
              all_goals try simp
            · -- Show that (fun t : ℝ => t • y) is differentiable
              apply Differentiable.smul
              · -- Show that (fun t : ℝ => t) is differentiable
                apply differentiable_id
              all_goals try simp
          all_goals try simp
      exact h2
    exact h1
  exact g'_diff
